title: Witch Week - Chapter 2 (Fragment 2)
text:
Miss Phillips was beside her. “Come on, Nan. Stand up on the knot.”

Somehow, feeling it was superhuman of her, Nan managed to lever herself upright. She stood there, wobbling gently around in little circles, while Miss Phillips, her face level with Nan’s trembling knees, kindly and patiently explained for the hundredth time exactly how to climb a rope.

Nan clenched her teeth. She would do it. Everyone else did. It must be possible. She shut her eyes to shut out the other girls’ grinning faces and did as Miss Phillips told her. She took a strong and careful grip on the rope above her head. Carefully, she put the rope between the top of one foot and the bottom of the other. She kept her eyes shut. Firmly, she pulled with her arms. Crisply, she pulled her feet up behind. Gripped again. Reached up again, with fearful concentration. Yes, this was it! She was doing it at last! The secret must be to keep your eyes shut. She gripped and pulled. She could feel her body easily swinging upward toward the ceiling, just as the others did it.

But, around her, the giggles grew to laughter, and the laughter grew into screams, then shouts, and became a perfect storm of hilarity. Puzzled, Nan opened her eyes. All around her, at knee level, she saw laughing red faces, tears running out of eyes, and people doubled over yelling with mirth. Even Miss Phillips was biting her lip and snorting a little. And small wonder. Nan looked down to find her gym shoes still resting on the knot at the bottom of the rope. After all that climbing, she was still standing on the knot.

Nan tried to laugh too. She was sure it had been very funny. But it was hard to be amused. Her only consolation was that, after that, none of the other girls could climb the ropes either. They were too weak with laughing.

The boys, meanwhile, were running around and around the field. They were stripped to little pale blue running shorts and splashing through the dew in big spiked shoes. It was against the rules to run in anything but spikes. They were divided into little groups of laboring legs. The quick group of legs in front, with muscles, belonged to Simon Silverson and his friends, and to Brian Wentworth. Brian was a good runner in spite of his short legs. Brian was prudently trying to keep to the rear of Simon, but every so often the sheer joy of running overcame him and he went ahead. Then he would get bumped and jostled by Simon’s friends, for everyone knew it was Simon’s right to be in front.

The group of legs behind these were paler and moved without enthusiasm. These belonged to Dan Smith and his friends. All of them could have run at least as fast as Simon Silverson, but they were saving themselves for better things. They loped along easily, chatting among themselves. Today, they kept bursting into laughter.

Behind these again labored an assorted group of legs: mauve legs, fat legs, bright white legs, legs with no muscles at all, and the great brown legs of Nirupam Singh, which seemed too heavy for the rest of Nirupam’s skinny body to lift. Everyone in the group was too breathless to talk. Their faces wore assorted expressions of woe.

The last pair of legs, far in the rear, belonged to Charles Morgan. There was nothing particularly wrong with Charles’s legs, except that his feet were in ordinary school shoes and soaked through. He was always behind. He chose to be. This was one of the few times in the day when he could be alone to think. He had discovered that, as long as he was thinking of something else, he could keep up his slow trot for hours. And think. The only interruptions he had to fear were when the other groups came pounding past him and he was tangled up in their efforts for a few seconds. Or when Mr. Towers, encased in his nice warm tracksuit, came loping up alongside and called ill-advised encouragements to Charles.

vocabulary.definition: Consisting of various kinds mixed together.
vocabulary.example: Behind these again labored an assorted group of legs: mauve legs, fat legs, bright white legs, legs with no muscles at all, and the great brown legs of Nirupam Singh, which seemed too heavy for the rest of Nirupam’s skinny body to lift.

vocabulary.definition: Unable to breathe easily or quickly, typically from exertion.
vocabulary.example: Everyone in the group was too breathless to talk.

vocabulary.definition: To break open or apart suddenly and violently, often figuratively meaning to suddenly show strong emotion.
vocabulary.example: Today, they kept bursting into laughter.

vocabulary.definition: To close tightly, especially fists or teeth, often showing determination or anger.
vocabulary.example: Nan clenched her teeth.

vocabulary.definition: The action of focusing one's attention.
vocabulary.example: Reached up again, with fearful concentration.

vocabulary.definition: Comfort received by a person after a loss or disappointment.
vocabulary.example: Her only consolation was that, after that, none of the other girls could climb the ropes either.

vocabulary.definition: Enclose or cover something completely.
vocabulary.example: Towers, encased in his nice warm tracksuit, came loping up alongside and called ill-advised encouragements to Charles.

vocabulary.definition: Words or actions that give someone confidence or support.
vocabulary.example: Towers, encased in his nice warm tracksuit, came loping up alongside and called ill-advised encouragements to Charles.

vocabulary.definition: Intense and eager enjoyment or interest.
vocabulary.example: The group of legs behind these were paler and moved without enthusiasm.

vocabulary.definition: The look on someone's face that shows emotions or thoughts.
vocabulary.example: Their faces wore assorted expressions of woe.

vocabulary.definition: To laugh in a silly or nervous way.
vocabulary.example: But, around her, the giggles grew to laughter, and the laughter grew into screams, then shouts, and became a perfect storm of hilarity.

vocabulary.definition: A firm hold or grasp.
vocabulary.example: She took a strong and careful grip on the rope above her head.

vocabulary.definition: Extreme amusement, especially when expressed by laughter.
vocabulary.example: But, around her, the giggles grew to laughter, and the laughter grew into screams, then shouts, and became a perfect storm of hilarity.

vocabulary.definition: An act of stopping or hindering something temporarily.
vocabulary.example: The only interruptions he had to fear were when the other groups came pounding past him and he was tangled up in their efforts for a few seconds.

vocabulary.definition: To push, elbow, or bump against someone roughly, typically in a crowd.
vocabulary.example: Then he would get bumped and jostled by Simon’s friends, for everyone knew it was Simon’s right to be in front.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of great pleasure and happiness.
vocabulary.example: Brian was prudently trying to keep to the rear of Simon, but every so often the sheer joy of running overcame him and he went ahead.

vocabulary.definition: Done with difficulty or effort.
vocabulary.example: Behind these again labored an assorted group of legs: mauve legs, fat legs, bright white legs, legs with no muscles at all, and the great brown legs of Nirupam Singh, which seemed too heavy for the rest of Nirupam’s skinny body to lift.

vocabulary.definition: To move or raise something using a lever or leverage.
vocabulary.example:
”

Somehow, feeling it was superhuman of her, Nan managed to lever herself upright.

vocabulary.definition: To run or move with long bounding strides.
vocabulary.example: They loped along easily, chatting among themselves.

vocabulary.definition: Amusement, especially expressed in laughter.
vocabulary.example: All around her, at knee level, she saw laughing red faces, tears running out of eyes, and people doubled over yelling with mirth.

vocabulary.definition: To successfully deal with or gain control over something difficult.
vocabulary.example: Brian was prudently trying to keep to the rear of Simon, but every so often the sheer joy of running overcame him and he went ahead.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows careful good judgment that avoids risks.
vocabulary.example: Brian was prudently trying to keep to the rear of Simon, but every so often the sheer joy of running overcame him and he went ahead.

vocabulary.definition: To cause liquid to scatter and fall in drops or waves.
vocabulary.example: They were stripped to little pale blue running shorts and splashing through the dew in big spiked shoes.

vocabulary.definition: To remove clothing or covering from.
vocabulary.example: They were stripped to little pale blue running shorts and splashing through the dew in big spiked shoes.

vocabulary.definition: Having or showing extraordinary powers or abilities beyond what is normal for a human.
vocabulary.example:
”

Somehow, feeling it was superhuman of her, Nan managed to lever herself upright.

vocabulary.definition: A set of loose-fitting clothes worn for exercise or sports.
vocabulary.example: Towers, encased in his nice warm tracksuit, came loping up alongside and called ill-advised encouragements to Charles.

vocabulary.definition: Shaking involuntarily, typically as a result of anxiety or excitement.
vocabulary.example: She stood there, wobbling gently around in little circles, while Miss Phillips, her face level with Nan’s trembling knees, kindly and patiently explained for the hundredth time exactly how to climb a rope.

vocabulary.definition: To move unsteadily from side to side.
vocabulary.example: She stood there, wobbling gently around in little circles, while Miss Phillips, her face level with Nan’s trembling knees, kindly and patiently explained for the hundredth time exactly how to climb a rope.
